title: Wage Growth vs Inflation Tracker 2026 | Real Income Data
description: Track real wage growth vs inflation in 2026. See if your salary is keeping up with CPI across sectors, education levels, and income percentiles.

schemas.headline: Wage Growth vs Inflation Tracker 2026
schemas.description: Track real wage growth vs inflation in 2026. See if your salary is keeping up with CPI across sectors, education levels, and income percentiles.

acceptedAnswer.text: Real wage growth is the increase in wages adjusted for inflation. It is calculated by subtracting the inflation rate (CPI) from the nominal wage growth rate.

mainEntity.name: Are wages keeping up with inflation in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Currently, real wage growth is at a positive rate of 1.18% year-over-year, meaning wages are outpacing inflation.

mainEntity.name: How is the wage growth vs inflation tracker calculated?

acceptedAnswer.text: The tracker uses data from the Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED), comparing the Consumer Price Index (CPI) with Average Hourly Earnings across various sectors, income percentiles, and education levels to calculate real wage growth.

mainEntity.name: Which sectors or percentiles have the highest real wage growth?

acceptedAnswer.text: Historically, sectors like Leisure & Hospitality, and lower income quartiles, have seen significant fluctuations. Our data table allows you to filter and sort real wage growth by specific sectors, education levels, and percentiles.

mainEntity.name: Why is my salary vs inflation comparison important?

acceptedAnswer.text: Tracking your salary against inflation shows your actual purchasing power. If inflation outpaces your salary increases, your effective income decreases.
